description: Compute the H2 molecule ground-state energy using VQE (Variational Quantum Eigensolver) with cqlib, compared against exact classical diagonalization across bond lengths.

inputs.hamiltonian_source: Qiskit Nature / PySCF standard output (g_II, g_IZ, g_ZI, g_ZZ, g_XX coefficients)

outputs.energies: ground-state total energy (Hartree) = electronic + nuclear repulsion V_nn

primary_metric_definition: |VQE_total - classical_exact_total| in mHartree; chemical accuracy threshold = 1.6 mHa
quantum_method: VQE with hardware-efficient ansatz (RY-RZ + CNOT), COBYLA optimizer, cqlib StatevectorSimulator
classical_baseline: Exact diagonalization of the 4x4 Pauli Hamiltonian (numpy.linalg.eigvalsh)
success_signals: VQE achieves chemical accuracy (< 1.6 mHartree error) at equilibrium bond length 0.74 Å
VQE energy tracks the exact potential energy curve across bond lengths
VQE converges within iterative layer budget (≤3 ansatz layers)
